headline: USA RARE EARTH GOES BIG: $2.8B Deal for Brazil's Pela Ema Mine Would Double TREO Production Base
snippet: USA Rare Earth (Nasdaq: USAR) agreed to acquire Serra Verde Group — owner of Brazil's Pela Ema rare earth mine — for $300M cash plus 126.8M new shares in a deal valued at approximately $2.8 billion. The transaction adds ~6,400 metric tonnes of TREO annual production capacity and projected run-rate EBITDA of $550–$650M by end-2027, closing expected Q3 2026.

headline: 35.3% TREO PEAK GRADES: Brazilian Rare Earths Posts Monster Intercepts at Monte Alto Ahead of JORC Resource
snippet: Brazilian Rare Earths (ASX: BRE) Q1 2026 quarterly report reveals exceptional Monte Alto drilling: 27.6m at 19.4% TREO and 23.9m at 17.4% TREO, with peak grades to 35.3% TREO and NdPr up to 5.96%. Sensor-based ore sorting achieves >2x grade upgrade with ~97% recovery — all ahead of a mid-2026 JORC resource estimate on a 32,372m-drilled project.

headline: VMS SYSTEM BUILDS: GreenLight Metals Hits 30.7m of 2.49% CuEq at Bend — Every 2026 Hole in Sulfide
snippet: GreenLight Metals (TSX-V: GRL) intersected 30.7m at 2.49% CuEq (1.05% Cu, 1.34 g/t Au) including 14.9m at 3.65% CuEq at the Bend VMS deposit in Wisconsin. Every hole drilled in the 2026 program has encountered significant massive-to-semi-massive sulfide, extending the system down-dip and along strike with three more assays still pending.

headline: 408-METRE INTERVAL: Neotech Metals Expands Hecla-Kilmer Rare Earth-Gallium System, Maiden Resource Due 2026
snippet: Neotech Metals (CSE: NTMC) returned 408.4m at 0.53% TREO, 4.2% P2O5, 21.5 g/t Ga2O3 and 0.13% Nb2O5 at the Hecla-Kilmer project in Ontario — a wide, gallium-and-niobium-enriched interval that reinforces the bulk-tonnage case ahead of a 2026 maiden resource estimate from the 10,000m-drilled campaign.

headline: 17.95 g/t GOLD OVER 22 METRES: Cambria Hits High-Grade at BC's Historic Premier District, Spins Out US Copper
snippet: Cambria Gold Mines reported initial infill drilling at Premier Gold in northwest British Columbia: 22m at 17.95 g/t gold, including a visible gold hit of 552 g/t over 0.5m. Separately, the company announced a plan to spin out its US copper project as a standalone vehicle.

headline: GOLD WHIPLASH AT $4,700: Iran Ceasefire Frays, Strait of Hormuz Stays Shut, FOMC Decision Due This Week
snippet: Gold slipped back below $4,700 as the US–Iran ceasefire showed cracks and the Strait of Hormuz remained effectively closed — before recovering on a new Iranian proposal — leaving the metal on track for its first down week in five. Silver held at $75.67/oz. Goldman Sachs maintains its $5,400 year-end gold target; J.P. Morgan projects $6,000–$6,300.

headline: SIXTH YEAR RUNNING: Silver Deficit Seen at 46.3 Moz in 2026, China March Imports 173% Above Seasonal Average
snippet: The Silver Institute's World Silver Survey 2026 (released April 15) forecasts a sixth consecutive annual deficit of 46.3 Moz, with China's March silver imports running 173% above the 10-year seasonal average as retail investors and solar manufacturers front-loaded supply ahead of China's VAT export rebate removal. Visible inventories across COMEX, LBMA and Shanghai have shed nearly 1 billion ounces since 2021.

headline: LITHIUM DOUBLED IN Q1: Battery-Grade Carbonate Hits Two-Year High, Up 155% Year-Over-Year After China's VAT Exit
snippet: Battery-grade lithium carbonate nearly doubled to US$26,278/tonne in Q1 2026 and sits 155% above year-ago levels, driven by China removing VAT export rebates on lithium-ion batteries — triggering a global production rush. SQM's 2026 EPS is forecast to surge over 100% and Albemarle shares hit two-year highs as the sub-$15,000 lithium era appears to be over.

headline: UR-ENERGY FLIPS THE SWITCH: Shirley Basin ISR Uranium Mine Goes Live in Wyoming as Second U.S. Operation
snippet: Ur-Energy (NYSE: URG) commenced uranium mining operations at Shirley Basin in Wyoming on April 23, capturing uranium-bearing solution from Mine Unit 1. The ISR project carries a licensed capacity of 2.0 Mlb U₃O₈/year with 9.1 Mlb in M&I resources over a nine-year mine life — raising the company's combined licensed capacity to 4.2 Mlb/year alongside Lost Creek.

headline: COT WATCH: April 24 Positioning Data for Gold, Silver and USD Index — Reading the Tape Into FOMC Week
snippet: GoldSeek's weekly Commitment of Traders breakdown covers the April 22 reporting week for gold, silver and the US Dollar Index — providing a read on how large speculators and commercial hedgers are positioned heading into the Fed's rate decision and the ongoing Iran geopolitical overhang.

headline: FED ON HOLD UNTIL LATE 2026: War-Driven Inflation Pushes Back Rate Cut Timeline, Reuters Poll Finds
snippet: A Reuters poll of economists finds the Federal Reserve's first rate cut has been pushed back to late 2026, as war-related inflation pressures — particularly oil price spikes from the Strait of Hormuz closure — keep monetary policy tighter for longer. For gold mining equities, this delays the discount-rate tailwind that analysts have been expecting.
